key_insight: A physically-constrained calibration framework effectively reconstructs thermal models from sparse temperature data for spacecraft applications.
engineering_relevance: This methodology enhances the accuracy and efficiency of thermal model calibration, crucial for spacecraft thermal management systems.
method: Utilizes trajectory-based inverse problem formulation for graph dynamical systems.
Imposes physical constraints on thermal model parameters to ensure stability and relevance.
Validates through synthetic data generated from high-fidelity finite element simulations.
evidence_signal: The calibrated Lumped Parameter Thermal Models (LPTMs) accurately reproduce long-term temperature evolution even in the presence of measurement noise.
watch_item: The reliance on synthetic datasets may limit the applicability to real-world scenarios without further validation.
paper_title: Physics-constrained identification of graph-based thermal networks for spacecraft digital twins
